restored_match.note: Slug says 'gremio-athletico' but home_team is Athletico in DB. Slug is reversed but data is consistent — match record has Athletico as home and Grêmio as away. TASK said Atlético-PR vs Grêmio (we confirmed Athletico-PR = e2902173 via sofascore_id 1967).

predictions_match_id_fk.definition: FOREIGN KEY (match_id) REFERENCES matches(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

points_history_prediction_id_fk.definition: FOREIGN KEY (prediction_id) REFERENCES predictions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL

delete_window_utc.evidence: Last scoring event in points_history at 23:51:31 (description='Match gremio-athletico finished'); restored match row created_at=23:57:29
delete_window_utc.log_evidence: Postgres logs only retained last ~24h (2026-05-03 noise only). API logs same. No DELETE statement traced.

scoring_state_at_delete.winners_goals_inference: Match ended 0-0. Awards were 2pts (correct_result), not 5pts (exact_score). So none predicted exactly 0-0. Each winner predicted home_team_goals=N and away_team_goals=N with N>=1 (any draw N-N). Cannot determine N without PITR.
scoring_state_at_delete.losing_predictions_lost: Other matches in round 14 had 7-8 predictions each. This match likely had 1-2 additional losing predictions (winner!=draw or wrong) which yielded 0pts and have no points_history orphan. Identities unknown without PITR.

user_profile_counter_drift.summary: Counters drifted BEFORE this incident (separate bug). For 4 of 6 affected users, current actual correct_predictions / total points exceeds profile values, which can't be caused by the delete (delete only removes rows). predictions_count matches exactly though.
user_profile_counter_drift.implication: Recovery prompt 4 must NOT recompute counters from scratch — that would also fix this pre-existing drift and obscure the recovery delta. Apply only the delta from recovery.

pitr_check_attempt: list_branches MCP returned 'Project reference is missing when validating permissions' — likely indicates branching/PITR not enabled on this plan. No backup-listing tool exposed via MCP.

partial_recovery_feasible_without_pitr.scope: Only the 6 winning predictions; goals set to a chosen draw value N-N (N>=1) since exact 0-0 is excluded by points_history (those would be 5pts). Losing predictions cannot be recovered.
partial_recovery_feasible_without_pitr.alternative: Manually ask each affected user to re-enter their prediction (best fidelity).

side_effects_to_watch: standings_cache last_updated_at=2026-05-03T18:03:49+00:00 — cache already refreshed post-incident, no action needed.
audit_logs table has 0 rows — no audit trail of the admin delete.

notifications.related_match_id ON DELETE not visible in our query but unlikely to be CASCADE; match row was recreated so any old notifications point at new UUID via slug match? No — recreated row has new UUID, old notifications would have been orphaned (related_match_id=NULL or also cascaded). Skip — out of scope.
blocking_questions_for_user: 1. Is Supabase PITR enabled on this project? (Free tier = NO, daily backups only with restore-to-new-project. Pro tier = optional add-on.)
2. If no PITR: partial recovery (6 winning predictions only, goals set to placeholder N-N) acceptable, or prefer asking users to re-enter their predictions?
3. If no PITR and going partial: which N? 1-1 default? Or ask each user individually?
